search_terms_used: super shoe running economy research pubmed
Nike Vaporfly running economy peer-reviewed study
running shoe cushioning injury prevention systematic review
minimalist shoe running economy biomechanics pubmed
running shoe drop heel-toe biomechanics injury study
coverage_notes: Strong coverage across carbon plate shoes (meta-analysis + landmark Hoogkamer RCT + Barnes crossover + Hebert-Losier review), cushioning and injury (Malisoux RCT with 848 runners), minimalist shoes (Fuller systematic review/meta-analysis + Ridge RCT + Fuller crossover), and general footwear constructions (Sun systematic review). Population mix includes elite, highly-trained, and recreational runners. Observational population-level data covered by Senefeld 2021 (3886 performances). All 6 search terms used. Gap: no dedicated heel-to-toe drop RCT included — existing drop studies are smaller and less cited; the Sun 2020 systematic review covers drop as a sub-topic.

papers.title: Metabolic effects of carbon-plated running shoes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.journal: Frontiers in Sports and Active Living
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 14 crossover trials examining metabolic effects of carbon-plated vs non-plated running shoes.

papers.title: The effect of footwear on running performance and running economy in distance runners
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 19 studies (14 in meta-analysis) examining how shoe mass, cushioning, and stiffness affect running economy and performance.

papers.title: A systematic review of the effect of running shoes on running economy, performance and biomechanics: analysis by brand and model
papers.notes: Systematic review of 36 articles analyzing 61 shoe models across brands for effects on running economy, performance, and biomechanics.

papers.title: Systematic Review of the Role of Footwear Constructions in Running Biomechanics: Implications for Running-Related Injury and Performance
papers.journal: Journal of Sports Science and Medicine
papers.notes: Systematic review of 63 studies examining how shoe constructions (midsole, heel-toe drop, minimalist design, lacing, heel flare) affect running biomechanics and injury.

papers.title: A Comparison of the Energetic Cost of Running in Marathon Racing Shoes
papers.notes: Compared energetic cost of running in Nike Vaporfly 4% prototypes versus two established marathon racing shoes in high-caliber runners.

papers.title: A Randomized Crossover Study Investigating the Running Economy of Highly-Trained Male and Female Distance Runners in Marathon Racing Shoes versus Track Spikes
papers.notes: Randomized crossover comparing running economy in Nike Vaporfly 4% vs track spikes vs Adidas Adios in highly-trained male and female distance runners.

papers.title: Metabolic and performance responses of male runners wearing 3 types of footwear: Nike Vaporfly 4%, Saucony Endorphin racing flats, and their own shoes
papers.journal: Journal of Sport and Health Science
papers.notes: Randomized crossover comparing metabolic cost and 5-km time-trial performance in Nike Vaporfly 4%, Saucony Endorphin racing flats, and habitual shoes in recreational male runners.

papers.title: Shoe Cushioning Influences the Running Injury Risk According to Body Mass: A Randomized Controlled Trial Involving 848 Recreational Runners
papers.notes: RCT examining whether shoe cushioning level (soft vs hard) influences running injury risk, stratified by runner body mass.

papers.title: Effects of a minimalist shoe on running economy and 5-km running performance
papers.notes: Experimental crossover comparing running economy and 5-km time-trial performance in minimalist versus conventional running shoes in trained runners.

papers.title: Technological advances in elite marathon performance
papers.notes: Observational analysis of 3886 performances from top 50 male and female marathoners at four major marathons to quantify shoe technology effects on race times.
